name: memorybridge
description: Persistent, local, token-budget-aware memory for AI assistants. Memories are stored in SQLite on your machine; the optional ingestion pipeline uses the DeepSeek/Anthropic APIs (and optionally Notion) only when configured.
long_description: MemoryBridge gives AI assistants a persistent memory that lives on your computer. Memories are stored locally in SQLite with FTS5 search, decay scoring, and automatic pruning to stay within token budgets. Supports multiple profiles, cross-model export (ChatGPT, Gemini, Ollama), and a portable Memory Passport format for pasting into any AI context window.

tools.name: get_memory
tools.description: Retrieve relevant memories within a token budget

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Add a new memory with automatic token counting and dedup

tools.name: add_memories
tools.description: Batch-add multiple facts in one operation

tools.name: edit_memory
tools.description: Edit an existing memory in place by memory_id

tools.name: search_memory
tools.description: Search memories using FTS5 BM25 full-text search

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Delete a specific memory by ID

tools.name: get_token_stats
tools.description: Get token usage statistics across profiles

tools.name: prune_memories
tools.description: Prune low-score memories to free token budget

tools.name: export_passport
tools.description: Export memory as a portable plain-text Memory Passport

tools.name: export_for_model
tools.description: Export memory formatted for ChatGPT, Gemini, or Ollama

tools.name: get_access_log
tools.description: Retrieve access log with token usage history

tools.name: get_prune_queue
tools.description: Get pending prune candidates awaiting review

tools.name: resolve_prune_queue
tools.description: Approve or reject a queued prune candidate
